evidence: Single government letter pulled Fable 5 and Mythos globally; 7+ source categories covering legal, commercial, developer, and sovereignty dimensions
Agent engineering crossed from tutorials to production operations with premortems, CI evals, FSM fallback patterns, and multi-tool workflows
API bills exploding 4x without new features shipped; models costing 8.6x more than expected; Baseten raising $1.5B confirms cost is now a market
51-point article on AI wrongly flagging human writing as low quality; AI detectors failing students; two-thirds of Americans think AI advancing too fast
HN hiring thread 242pts/502 comments; founders stopping junior hiring; Pragmatic Engineer two-part job market analysis for 2026
831-point article on 10k GitHub repos distributing Trojan malware; Copilot 2FA vulnerability; Bitwarden CLI compromised; attack sequences bypassing individual permission checks

GLM-5.2 called most powerful open weights text LLM; cloud-to-local compute debate intensified by Fable provider shutdown
India debating AI sovereignty; export controls creating national AI stacks; Korean telecom impact at 115 points
Developers running Claude Code and Codex side by side without embarrassment; multi-provider becoming the default after Fable shutdown
Tom Johnson on judging beautiful docs, AI fatigue, and tool slop; dev.to covering professional documentation journey and writing advice
SpaceX acquires Cursor for $60B; Baseten $1.5B; Elastic acquires DeductiveAI $85M; OpenAI IPO prep; money flowing to AI infrastructure and reliability
Using premortems with Claude and Codex before shipping; 40-point article on a new workflow pattern for agent-assisted development
Non-engineer executive built B2B SaaS to production in two days with Claude Code; then the team found the exposed API key
Put your agent evals in CI or stop calling them evals; engineering practice emerging for agent quality assurance
Stateful provider fallback using finite state machine pattern for LLM pipelines; practical architecture for multi-provider resilience
AI coding debt identified as qualitatively different from traditional tech debt; smelly config files giving agents bad instructions
Was 1545 engagement on June 11; zero items across 311 this cycle; dead signal
Was 664 (Jun 11) to 1333 (Jun 18) as standalone theme; absorbed into larger governance crisis this cycle
Confirmed fatigue across sources; content shifted entirely from replacement narrative to operational reality and practitioner concerns

Contrarian cut of [[ai_coding_debt]]: 91/100 repos have smelly configs (context bloat 42%, lint leakage 62%); agent measurably dumber mid-session as window fills; 'SKILLS.md is enough is exactly backwards'. More context = worse, contradicts the bigger-CLAUDE.md consensus

Contrarian cut of [[ai_cost_reliability_pain]]: cheaper-sticker model cost 8.6x more in practice; 3300 paired tests show bills don't match model names; GLM-5.2 token-hungry at 43k tokens/task. Refutes route-to-cheap/model-cascade orthodoxy

Sharper practitioner cut of [[open_weights_local_ai]]: Boykis 'running local models is good now', Gerganov uses Qwen3.6-27B daily, GLM-5.2 beats closed models on Code Arena WebDev. Contradicts frontier-cloud-supremacy assumption; Fable ban accelerates

Former contrarian take now becoming consensus: 'AI demands more discipline not less' (Charity Majors), GitClear 8x duplication + refactoring 25%->10%, DORA 7.2% stability drop. Contrarian edge fading; only a meta-take survives

'Agents write code but they don't remember' (23p/35c): SDLC inverting, intent becomes the spine and code a layer you drill into. 'Coding Agents Made Me Take Specs Seriously' (11p/16c). 'I gave Claude Code a team' multi-agent orchestration. 'AI Is Too Good at Coding - So I Built Something to Keep the Why'. Polygraph ships cross-repo agent session memory. Spec/intent as source of truth.

'Everyone's Excited About Claude Tag. Nobody's Built the Trust Layer' (20p/25c). 'Trust Isn't a Scalar: Typed Provenance for Agent Chains' (13p/11c). 'Don't use an LLM to decide what your AI agent is allowed to do' (10p/11c). 'How My AI Agent Hacked Its Own Permissions' (15p/13c). Linux Foundation 'DNS-style trust for AI agents'. Agentic AI security headlined the Confidential Computing Summit. Prompt Injection as Role Confusion (Simon Willison + lobsters).

Last cycle's contrarian take is now mainstream: Pragmatic Engineer ran THREE pieces ('Slow down to speed up', 'Ideas: slow down to speed up with AI agents', job-market deepdive) on devs generating 2x code with falling quality. '80/20 Rule of AI Code: last 20% takes 80%' (35p/21c). 'Everyone Is Building AI Agents: Nobody Talks About the Technical Debt'. 'Maybe It Is Not Yet Time To Bring Every AI Demo To Production' (7p/9c).

'AI coding token costs are on track to rival human payroll' (infoworld). Pragmatic Engineer 'trend of trying to cut back on AI spend within eng departments'. 'I Spent $8,857 Using Claude Code to Build 6 Projects'. 'Five ways your AI coding agent wastes tokens'. AI Gateway posts (devto-claude, devto-llm, 'Ask HN: Best AI Gateway?'). Hetzner doubled prices ('AI memory crunch'). $340 surprise AWS bill.

Platformer ran nearly a whole issue: AWS CEO hiring 11k juniors vs founder who stopped hiring juniors, 'Claude Code's creator on the end of the software engineer', economist's case against jobs-pocalypse. Pragmatic Engineer 'State of the SE job market 2026 part 2'. HN 'Who is hiring?' 242p/501c; 'Big AI labs are hiring philosophers' 147p/133c. 'I shipped 150+ PRs and built agents in a day - but I still can't get a job'.

Lenny's 'GLM 5.2: why I'm replacing Opus in Claude Code with this new model' (open weight, cheaper). 'Sipp: a local-first runtime for Hybrid AI Applications'. Self-hosting on Jetson Orin Nano + Ollama. Lobsters 'fully local voice assistant'. Infoworld 'air-gapped AI model mode in VS Code'. 'DeepSeek Flash inverted the economics of agent products' (HN show).

Anthropic accuses Alibaba of 'largest AI distillation attack to date' — 28.8M fraudulent / 25,000 fake accounts to scrape Claude (hn-beat x6, infoworld). 'Chinese A.I. Models Close the Gap with Anthropic and OpenAI'. The $27M Anthropic-vs-OpenAI political proxy war over Alex Bores ends in a draw (verge-ai). IP extraction / model-distillation becomes a legal and geopolitical front.

'Too cheap to be good? Think again' (63p/93c, top practitioner post this cycle): two-phase benchmark with external review, 'the winning model? Not the one you'd expect'. Confirms last week's contrarian cut that cheaper-sticker models cost more in practice.

'I trusted my CLAUDE.md. WordPress.org rejected the exact thing it was supposed to prevent' (6p/5c). 'Context Compaction Visualizer: see exactly what your AI agent forgot'. 'Stop Wasting Tokens: how to structure Claude.md'. 'Every rule I add to my setup has to kill an old one' — the anti-bloat discipline emerging.

Tom Johnson 'Developing internal skills for recurring documentation processes like release notes' (agent skills for docs) and 'Judging beautiful docs, AI fatigue, and tool slop'. 'To The People Who Read My Weird Little Blogs' (54p/31c). 'AI Agents For Release Notes And Changelog Automation'. Docs craft + skills-for-docs persists.

'AI text detectors are mostly guessing: how they actually work'. 'The 47 Words That Make Your Text Sound AI-Written'. Mollick 'Choosing to Stay Human'. 'GEO: How to Get Your Content Cited by AI Search Engines (Princeton study)'. The human-vs-machine writing-quality debate persists at lower heat.

Braintrust on Lenny's: 'Evals are the modern version of a PRD'. 'An AI Feature Has No Tests Pass Moment. So I Write the Eval First' (13p/25c). 'Auto-verifying your AI-SRE's fixes: HolmesGPT end-to-end on a real cluster' (18p/1c). Eval-first practice maturing.

'Git Worktrees + Claude Code: Run Parallel AI Agents on the Same Repo (2026)' and GitHub Blog 'What are git worktrees, and why should I use them?'. 'I Ran Claude Code on Every New Claude Model'. Multi-agent / parallel-isolation workflows surfacing as a named pattern.

Last cycle's FSM-fallback pattern is productizing into 'AI gateways': 'LLM Gateways: Routing, Fallbacks, And Semantic Caching' (10p/1c), 'What Is an AI Gateway? (the week we realized we needed one)', 'Ask HN: Best AI Gateway?'. The routing/fallback layer is becoming a category.

GitHub Blog: 'Evaluating the Copilot agentic harness across models', 'Getting more from each token: context handling and model routing', 'Copilot CLI for Beginners: slash commands'. Infoworld 'Making Windows a developer platform, again'. Steady Copilot-CLI investment as Claude Code competitor.

Changelog: 'Bitwarden CLI compromised' (Checkmarx supply-chain campaign), 'LiteLLM got hit by a nasty supply-chain attack', 'Astral acquired by OpenAI'. Ars: 'Massive breach spills credentials for thousands of networks', 'Crypto Clipper backdoor spreads over USB'. Supply-chain attacks persist.

White House asks OpenAI to slow-roll GPT-5.6 over safety (techcrunch + verge). Congresswoman denies staff used AI to write a defense funding amendment. 'The Trump White House is over Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei'. Anthropic sued the DoD. Government-vs-frontier-lab governance intensifies.

Menlo Ventures raises $3B for AI startups (largest in its history). Patronus AI $50M to stress-test agents. General Intuition $320M ($2.3B) training agents on gameplay. Robotics startups raised $18.8B in 2026. Money still pouring into AI infra, agent-testing, and reliability.

Anthropic's Claude Tag 'aims to turn workplace AI from a personal assistant into a teammate' (infoworld); Karpathy called it 'the third major' shift. The product framing (agents as colleagues) is new but coverage is thin — watch for accumulation.

'What Happens When We Stop Asking': Stack Overflow peaked at 200,000 questions/month in 2014, barely 3,000 in 2026. The worry is bigger than SO — where does the next generation's shared knowledge come from when everyone asks a private model.

The acute Fable shutdown crisis has decayed into routine product coverage ('Claude Fable arrives', 'What it feels like to work with Mythos') plus follow-ups ('Big implications of US banning Fable'). The energy migrated to the Alibaba distillation IP war — the crisis's successor story.
